instruction: Fill the telesales data into the statistics table

command: DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true

printf 'March 2025 Telesales Records
Sales Rep: Chen Jing
Total Calls: 128
Connected Calls: 89
Connection Rate: 69.5%
Interested Clients: 23
Signed Clients: 8
Signed Amount: 425K CNY' > /home/user/Desktop/sales_calls.txt

python3 -c "import openpyxl; wb = openpyxl.Workbook(); ws = wb.active; ws['A1'] = 'Sales Rep'; ws['B1'] = 'Total Calls'; ws['C1'] = 'Connected'; ws['D1'] = 'Connection Rate(%)'; ws['E1'] = 'Signed'; ws['F1'] = 'Signed Amount(10K CNY)'; wb.save('/home/user/Desktop/call_stats.xlsx')"

phases.instruction: Fill the telesales data into the statistics table
Fill the data from /home/user/Desktop/sales_calls.txt into /home/user/Desktop/call_stats.xlsx row 2: A2='Chen Jing', B2=128, C2=89, D2=69.5, E2=8, F2=42.5, save

result.command: python3 -c "import pandas as pd; df = pd.read_excel('/home/user/Desktop/call_stats.xlsx'); s = df.to_string(); print('PASSED' if 'Chen Jing' in s else 'FAILED')"
